title: Quiz: QCCD Architecture
description: Test your understanding of QCCD for scaling trapped ions

questions.question: In QCCD architecture, why are gate zones kept small (2-4 ions)?

options.text: To maintain high gate fidelity by avoiding mode crowding
Because lasers can only address a few ions

questions.explanation: Small gate zones avoid mode crowding and maintain clear mode structure, enabling high-fidelity entangling gates. Additional qubits are stored elsewhere and shuttled in as needed.

questions.question: What is a typical ion shuttling speed in QCCD systems?
questions.explanation: Ions are typically shuttled at 1-10 m/s, balancing speed (to minimize circuit time) with adiabaticity (to avoid motional excitation).

questions.question: What operation allows arbitrary two-qubit connectivity in QCCD?

options.text: Physical reordering of ions through splitting and recombining

questions.explanation: QCCD achieves arbitrary connectivity by physically reordering ions - splitting chains, rotating/swapping, and recombining - so any two ions can be brought adjacent for gates.
